Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Class Action Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-10-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF RANDY ARBUCKLE Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Class Action Complaint

No appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF RANDY ARBUCKLE'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Class Action Complaint, as follows:

The motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted.

Leave to amend is routinely granted. Morgan v. Superior Court 172 Cal. App. 2d 527, 530 (1959). Weil & Brown, et al., California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial 6:617, 6:653 (Rutter: 2022). The classic rule focuses on prejudice: "denying a request to amend a complaint may be appropriate when an unreasonable delay in seeking amendment prejudices the defendant." Payton, 27 Cal.App.5th at 849 (emphasis supplied). Defendant's claim of prejudice is conclusory and vague. Oppo. at 5:8-13.

The essence of the opposition is that a collective bargaining agreement (CBA) blocks some-but not all-of the potentially aggrieved employees from pursing the PAGA claim which is the subject of this motion. The issue is best handled by any response to the second amended complaint defendant wishes to make, in particular because defendant does not claim no employee may participate in a superior court PAGA claim. See e.g. Partridge Decl. dated February 27, 2023 at 4 ("majority" of employees are members of union).

Plaintiffs should file and serve the second amended complaint not later than 16 March, 2023.

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/CK)
